parsed_review.summary: CP00-220 is a descriptor-only DMS-core pack (risk A, 10 units, RP06.P06.M02.S21-RP06.P06.M03.S08) closing the RP06.P06.M02 Type-And-Shape tail (cross-tenant test, leak prevention test) and opening the M03 Primary Implementation Slice head (permission matrix row, view/search/mutation/export-download/share/AI-retrieval decision bindings, audit hint fields). Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claims are fully supported. Plan binding is consistent across closeout-pack-plan.json (live), manifest plan_binding_snapshot, review-baseline, registry DMS_CORE_CP220_PACK_BINDING, and the dms-core-contract.json projection (current_pack + permission_matrix_slice_descriptor + no_write_attestation): deliverable distribution {test:2, security_audit:2, implementation:6} and micro-title distribution {Type And Shape Definition:2, Primary Implementation Slice:8} match exactly; next pack CP00-221 / next subphase RP06.P06.M03.S09. The production_ready claim is NOT over-stated: manifest declares production_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review, the production_ready_flag is a milestone name, and the Hermes production_ready_candidate=true is a pre-human-review candidate only. The descriptor-only / no-write / no-real-data boundary holds at the code level — DMS source imports are all local (model/registry), with no fs/child_process/network/process.env/fetch, and all builder functions return Object.freeze'd plain data; validators enforce the negative flags (cross_tenant_access_allowed=false, leak_detected=false, dispatches_ai_runtime=false, executes_file_download=false, external_share_executed=false, audit_hint_detail_included=false, deny-over-allow descriptor enforced). The two security_audit deliverables are handled as descriptor rows with no permission-decision/audit-hint/audit-body detail exposure. Authority boundary is respected: Claude is a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob only), claude_is_final_approval=false everywhere, no enterprise-trust-from-local-validation, human final approval required to open runtime; Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic evidence packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). CP178+ review hardening is encoded via forbidden_review_evidence (auth_failure, stdout_0_bytes, malformed_json, fenced_json_plus_prose, tool_call_shaped_output, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_or_enterprise_trust_claim) and the readiness auth-preflight passed with well-formed JSON. Changed-file scope correctly isolates the 8 DMS implementation refs (index.js unchanged as it wildcard re-exports) and preserves the unrelated UI/API workstream dirty files without attributing them to this pack. The required post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are absent, which is expected since they are produced downstream after this review step. No P0/P1/P2/P3 defects found.
